attributes.content:
## Overview

The Timesheet card gives the workspace a structured home for weekly/monthly time entries aggregated by project with totals and approval status.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Timesheet acts as a canonical operational record. It keeps important facts, state changes, timestamps, and linked parties in one place so downstream workflows, approvals, and reporting can all rely on the same source of truth.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Contact]]** -- Timesheet often needs a person-level reference for ownership, communication, or accountability.
- **[[Organization]]** -- Timesheet frequently sits inside an account, company, or team-level relationship context.
- **[[Task]]** -- Timesheet often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Timesheet can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Document]]** -- Timesheet can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Interview Feedback]]** -- Interview Feedback can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Timesheet.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Timesheet as the structured home for weekly/monthly time entries aggregated by project with totals and approval status and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Timesheet when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Timesheet stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.

cardInfo.summary: Weekly/monthly time entries aggregated by project with totals and approval status.
